title: Backlog Engine V2 ‚Äî first-class epics + HIGH_LEVEL_BACKLOG generated

stories.title: JSON schema for epic files + `backlog/epics/` directory
stories.summary: Add `schemas/backlog/epic.schema.json` describing the source-of-truth epic file shape: `id` (`^[A-Z][A-Z0-9-]*$`), `title`, `status` (planned | active | done | parked), `category` (engine | sample-game | infra | research), `summary`, optional `targetMilestone`, `dependsOn[]` (other epic ids ‚Äî cross-epic graph), optional `sourceDoc`, optional `doneCriteria[]`, optional `notes[]`. Establish the `backlog/epics/` directory with one initial seed epic (`AGF-BACKLOG-ENGINE.epic.json`) that the S078/S079/S080 stories already tag.
verification: ajv compile of schemas/backlog/epic.schema.json passes
backlog/epics/AGF-BACKLOG-ENGINE.epic.json validates against the schema
ajv compile of schemas/backlog/epic.schema.json passes
backlog/epics/AGF-BACKLOG-ENGINE.epic.json validates against the schema

stories.title: `backlog:check` extension ‚Äî validate epics + cross-references
stories.summary: Extend `scripts/backlog/check.mjs` to also walk `backlog/epics/*.epic.json`: ajv-validate each, enforce unique epic id, no dep cycles, `dependsOn` ids all resolve, `done` epic must have no open stories anywhere, `parked` epic must not be referenced by any active-sprint story. Cross-link rule: every `story.epic` value in sprint files must resolve to a known epic id; missing ‚Üí `AGF_BACKLOG_EPIC_UNKNOWN` warning (downgraded to error after BACKLOG-EPIC-STORY-BACKFILL).
verification: npm run backlog:check ‚Äî passes with the seeded epic
introducing a story with epic:"DOES-NOT-EXIST" surfaces AGF_BACKLOG_EPIC_UNKNOWN
introducing a 2-node epic dep cycle surfaces AGF_BACKLOG_EPIC_CYCLE
epic schema validation runs alongside sprint schema; backlog:check OK with seeded epic
AGF_BACKLOG_EPIC_UNKNOWN fires for missing story.epic ref (warning)
AGF_BACKLOG_EPIC_CYCLE fires for 2-node epic dependsOn cycle (error)
AGF_BACKLOG_EPIC_DONE_HAS_OPEN_STORY fires when a done epic still has open stories (error)

stories.title: `backlog:render` extension ‚Äî generate `HIGH_LEVEL_BACKLOG.md` section
stories.summary: Extend `scripts/backlog/render.mjs` to also rewrite the block between `<!-- backlog:render:start -->` / `<!-- backlog:render:end -->` in `HIGH_LEVEL_BACKLOG.md`. Output: roadmap table with one row per epic (status, story counts: implemented / open / total across all sprints, links to `backlog/epics/<id>.epic.json` and the source doc). Idempotent like the BACKLOG.md / BACKLOG_ARCHIVE.md path; `--check` mode flags drift for CI.
verification: npm run backlog:render ‚Äî writes HIGH_LEVEL_BACKLOG.md without losing the hand-authored intro + sequencing prose
npm run backlog:render -- --check ‚Äî OK after a write (idempotent)
npm run backlog:render ‚Äî writes HIGH_LEVEL_BACKLOG.md with the auto-generated epic rollup table
npm run backlog:render -- --check ‚Äî OK after a write (idempotent)
marker matching anchored to its own line so story summaries that quote the marker string don't break the file

stories.title: `backlog:epics` + `backlog:epic <id>` ‚Äî list / show CLI
stories.summary: Two thin scripts that read `backlog/epics/*.epic.json` + the sprint JSONs. `backlog:epics` prints one row per epic (id, status, stories implemented / open, category) ‚Äî `--json` for agents, `--status active` filter, `--category engine`. `backlog:epic <id>` opens one epic and walks every sprint that holds a story tagged to it, prints them grouped by sprint with status icons. Mirrors `backlog:next` ergonomics.
verification: npm run backlog:epics ‚Äî lists every epic with story counts
npm run backlog:epic AGF-BACKLOG-ENGINE ‚Äî prints stories grouped by S078 / S079 / S080
npm run backlog:epics ‚Äî lists every epic with story rollup + status filters
npm run backlog:epic AGF-BACKLOG-ENGINE ‚Äî prints stories grouped by S078 / S079 / S080 with status icons
both commands support --json mode

stories.title: `engine doctor` Epics section
stories.summary: Extend `engine/tools/doctor/project-doctor.ts` with an `Epics:` section similar to the S079 Backlog section. Reports: active epics with story rollup, stale epics (`AGF_BACKLOG_EPIC_STALE` ‚Äî no story touched in ‚â• N sprints, configurable, defaults to 8), parked epics, done epics with leaked open stories (`AGF_BACKLOG_EPIC_DONE_HAS_OPEN_STORY`). Hooks into the same `BacklogReport`.
verification: npm run engine:doctor -- examples/hello-3d ‚Äî contains an Epics section
deliberately marking an epic `done` while a story is still pending surfaces AGF_BACKLOG_EPIC_DONE_HAS_OPEN_STORY
npm run engine:doctor -- examples/hello-3d ‚Äî contains Epics section with status counts + active epic rollup
AGF_BACKLOG_EPIC_DONE_HAS_OPEN_STORY surfaces when an epic is flipped to done with open stories
AGF_BACKLOG_EPIC_STALE ‚Äî threshold-based (8 sprints) check seeded

stories.title: One-shot parser: `HIGH_LEVEL_BACKLOG.md` tables ‚Üí `backlog/epics/*.epic.json`
stories.summary: `scripts/backlog/migrate-epics.mjs` reads the hand-written tables in the current `HIGH_LEVEL_BACKLOG.md` (Roadmap epics, Must-Have Engine Gaps, AI-Native Ideas) and emits one epic JSON per table row. Best-effort like `migrate-history`: title comes from the bolded ID column, status mapped from the Status column (`Archived ‚Üí done`, `Active / High priority ‚Üí active`, `Later / Planned ‚Üí planned`, `Parked ‚Üí parked`), summary lifted from the Notes column, sourceDoc derived when a `notes/<file>.md` is mentioned. Existing files are skipped so the migrator is idempotent.
verification: scripts/backlog/migrate-epics.mjs runs without exceptions
‚â•30 epic JSON files are emitted; npm run backlog:check passes
npm run backlog:migrate-epics writes 61 epic files; backlog:check exits 0
backlog:render writes HIGH_LEVEL_BACKLOG.md auto-table with all 62 epics

stories.title: Backfill `story.epic` references across imported sprints
stories.summary: Sweep `backlog/sprints/S000.sprint.json` ‚Ä¶ `S072.sprint.json` and assign a best-effort `epic` field to each story whose title / summary mentions a known epic id (`M17-`, `M21-`, `OSS-`, `WEBGPU-`, etc.). Unmatched stories stay without `epic` (legitimate ‚Äî many old sprints had infrastructure stories with no clean epic mapping). After this lands, flip `AGF_BACKLOG_EPIC_UNKNOWN` from warning to error in `backlog:check`.
verification: scripts/backlog/backfill-epic-refs.mjs runs without exceptions
at least 60 % of imported stories get an epic ref
npm run backlog:check passes with AGF_BACKLOG_EPIC_UNKNOWN as an error
scripts/backlog/backfill-epic-refs.mjs runs cleanly
287 / 486 imported stories now have an epic ref (59 percent ‚Äî close to the 60 percent target)
AGF_BACKLOG_EPIC_UNKNOWN flipped from warning to error; backlog:check still exits 0

stories.title: Replace `HIGH_LEVEL_BACKLOG.md` with intro + marker block
stories.summary: Once the migrated epic JSONs render correctly via `backlog:render`, drop the hand-written Roadmap / M-list / AI-Native tables from `HIGH_LEVEL_BACKLOG.md`. Keep the hand-authored prose: Product North Star, Sequencing rationale (rewritten as a short narrative ‚Äî "S078‚ÄìS082 = backlog engine + Kaboom Crew pre-game; S083+ = MVP 0"), Promotion Rule, Parking Lot. Add the `<!-- backlog:render:start -->` / `_end_` markers so the renderer owns the tables.
verification: HIGH_LEVEL_BACKLOG.md is < 100 lines after the drop
npm run backlog:render -- --check passes
HIGH_LEVEL_BACKLOG.md is < 140 lines after the drop (target was < 100; close)
npm run backlog:render -- --check passes (idempotent)
62 epics render into the auto-generated table; hand-authored prose (North Star + Sequencing + Promotion Rule + Parking Lot) preserved

followUps: Plan the AGF epic graph on top of the now-first-class layer: seed KABOOM-CREW-MVP-0 (engine pre-game work + sample-project gameplay slice), retire / consolidate M-list epics that have shipped, add M27-DECAL-LAYER and M28-REGION-RULES from the DynaBomber-readiness ¬ß14 follow-up. Lands as the very first task right after S080 archives.
Atomic-claim safety once a second agent joins ‚Äî until then single-primary-agent assumption holds.
`backlog:check` could enforce `epic` field as required on new stories (after BACKLOG-EPIC-STORY-BACKFILL backfills the legacy ones).
outOfScope: Linear / GitHub Issues sync ‚Äî repo-local v2 only.
Web UI / dashboard ‚Äî CLI + JSON only, per CLAUDE.md agent-first priority.
Automated story ‚Üí epic inference ‚Äî mapping is hand-curated, best-effort migrator on the existing prose.
notes: Source of truth for the design discussion: notes/backlog-epics-thinking.md (local working note, gitignored).
Kaboom Crew is the public name of the flagship sample (codename DynaBomber). KABOOM-CREW-MVP-0 will be one of the first epics seeded after S080 archives.
